mnemonics: DDL DML DCL DQL - Data Languages: Do Ducks Make Cool Quacks? (DDL-Define, DML-Manipulate, DCL-Control, DQL-Query)
The Three-Level Architecture: PILE (Physical, Internal, Logical, External) - Think of organizing a pile of books from the raw paper (Physical) to the detailed story (External).
keyPoints: DDL (Data Definition Language) is used to define database schemas (e.g., CREATE, ALTER).
DML (Data Manipulation Language) is used for managing data within schema objects (e.g., INSERT, UPDATE).
The Three-Schema Architecture (Internal, Conceptual, External) separates the physical database, the logical design, and the user views to provide data independence.
commonMistakes: Confusing DDL with DML; DDL defines the structure, while DML manipulates the data inside that structure.

Assuming the conceptual schema is the same as the external schema; the conceptual schema is the logical design for the entire database, while external schemas are tailored for specific user groups or applications.
